keyword.cluster: AI agent production deployment scaling enterprise
keyword.trend_momentum: accelerating — 80% of enterprises report measurable ROI from AI agents (Claude/KPMG 2026 data), NVIDIA Agent Toolkit launched March 2026, NIST AI Agent Standards Initiative active, Gartner: 40% of net-new enterprise apps will include task-specific agents by end of 2026. The bridge from 'pilot' to 'production at scale' is the defining enterprise challenge of H1 2026.
secondary: moving AI agents from pilot to production

selection_rationale.why_this_keyword: Enterprise AI Agent Deployment is the culmination of AgentForge's enterprise AI agent lifecycle series. Run #1 covered orchestration (how to build). Run #2 covered governance (how to control). Run #3 covered evaluation (how to measure). Run #4 covers deployment (how to scale into production). Together they form the complete framework: Build → Control → Measure → Deploy.
timeliness_factors: Claude/KPMG 2026 survey: 80% of enterprises report measurable ROI — the 'does it work?' question is answered, the 'how do we scale it?' question is now urgent
NVIDIA Agent Toolkit launch (March 16, 2026) — first major infrastructure vendor to ship agent-native deployment tooling
NIST AI Agent Standards Initiative — security and interoperability standards now under development, enterprises need guidance
Gartner: 40% of net-new enterprise apps will include task-specific agents by end of 2026 — deployment tooling is racing to catch up
85% of companies experiment with agentic AI, but the gap between 'experiment' and 'production at scale' is where value is captured or lost
Perfect capstone to AgentForge's lifecycle series: orchestration (May 23), governance (May 25), evaluation (May 26), deployment (May 27)
selection_rationale.domain_alignment: Perfect — AgentForge's core domain is AI agents and enterprise automation. This is the 'how to ship it' article that makes the previous three actionable. Highest commercial intent in the series.
selection_rationale.differentiation_from_prior_runs: Run #1 (May 23): AI Agent Orchestration — architectural patterns for multi-agent systems. Run #2 (May 25): Agentic AI Governance — control frameworks and compliance. Run #3 (May 26): AI Agent Evaluation — measuring and testing agent quality. Run #4 (today): Enterprise AI Agent Deployment — scaling from pilot to production, infrastructure, ops, and ROI. Each builds on the previous. No overlap.

article_plan.tone: Enterprise B2B, authoritative but accessible — learnings from Runs #1–3: target Flesch 35+, include 3+ plain-language interludes, embed hyperlinks from the start, meta description filled, title ≤60 chars, write article and meta description simultaneously
